summary: `Environment` ambient light is not affected by `CameraAttributes` exposure

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in v4.0.stable.official [92bee43ad] - Reproducible in v4.5.stable.official [876b29033] Reproducible with Forward+, Mobile, and Compatibility. ### System information Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5070 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8108) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-13700K (24 threads) - 63.74 GiB memory ### Issue description Changing `CameraAttributes` exposure settings does not affect ambient light from the `Environment` when the ambient light source is either `Color` or `Background` when Background mode is `Color`. Specifically, if the `CameraAttributes` exposure multiplier is set to `0.0` and there is ambient light (source: color or background color), the image is not black: This issue does not seem to present itself when the ambient light source is set to `Sky` or `Background` when Background mode is `Sky`. It happens with and without physical light units with both `CameraAttributesPractical` or `CameraAttributesPhysical`. ### Fog It seems that the same issue happens with the Fog environment effect, but not with Volumetric fog. I suspect there is a fair bit of review and testing of all of the features of Godot to fully resolve this issue... ### Expected behaviour: I would expect the `CameraAttributes` exposure to produce the same image as the tonemap exposure setting. I believe the only difference between these two exposure settings is that tonemap exposure can be applied to canvas items (via Background mode: Canvas), while CameraAttributes exposure cannot. Please correct me if my expectation is incorrect! ### Steps to reproduce 1. Create a 3D scene with a world environment 2. Set the ambient light source to Color and change the color to be non-black 3. Add a `CameraAttributes` to the environment 4. Change exposure settings 5. Note that ambient light appears unaffected. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [camera-attributes-ambient-light.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22729380/camera-attributes-ambient-light.zip)

summary: Pipeline cache is not saved when Android suspends application

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5.stable
- Likely reproducible in: 4.6.dev [60b7b8b16]

### System information

Godot v4.6.dev (60b7b8b16) - Fedora Linux 42 (Adams) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT (RADV GFX1201) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 60.39 GiB memory

### Issue description

There is currently no handling of application suspension/pausing/backgrounding (i.e. `NOTIFICATION_APPLICATION_PAUSED`) for saving out Godot's own pipeline cache, which becomes a problem on Android, since no engine cleanup/finalization happens when the app is eventually killed off in its suspended state, which the pipeline cache currently depends on.

This means that unless your pipeline cache exceeds the limit set by the `rendering/rendering_device/pipeline_cache/save_chunk_size_mb` project setting, which by default is set to 3 MB (a fairly high threshold by my measurements), you will never actually save the pipeline cache to disk, unless you exit the app "properly" by pressing the back button (or performing the back gesture) while `application/config/quit_on_go_back` is enabled.

(I would probably argue that most Android users don't close their apps by pressing the back button, and instead either let them be killed off by the OS while suspended, or explicitly do so by dismissing them from the Recents screen.)

This leads to needless stuttering from pipeline compilations that should rightfully have been cached.

What likely needs to happen is that code for saving out the pipeline cache needs to be added to `Java_org_godotengine_godot_GodotLib_onRendererPaused` here:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/60b7b8b16e07b344117fc33525f31d65c499cb22/platform/android/java_godot_lib_jni.cpp#L600-L608

### Steps to reproduce

1. Set up any project (using the Mobile renderer) for debugging in Android Studio (i.e. [this guide](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/engine_details/development/configuring_an_ide/android_studio.html)).
2. Put a breakpoint in `RenderingDevice::_update_pipeline_cache`.
3. Debug the app from Android Studio, on any Android device.
4. Close the app by dismissing it from the Recents screen.
5. Note how `RenderingDevice::_update_pipeline_cache` is never called with `p_closing` set to `true`.
6. Debug the app from Android Studio again.
7. Close the app by pressing the back button (or doing the back gesture).
8. Note how `RenderingDevice::_update_pipeline_cache` is called with `p_closing` set to `true`, from `RenderingDevice::finalize`.

(I suppose you can do this with some "print debugging" as well, without having to deal with Android Studio.)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: Disconnect ScriptEditor script list from `tree_changed` signal

description.text:
ScriptEditor does not need to be connected to `tree_changed`.
`_update_script_names` is called everywhere that the script list actually needs to update, when a script opens / moves / closes, or on filesystem update, etc.

With `--debug-canvas-item-redraw` you can see the script list updating every time the scene tree changes, like with every tooltip that opens anywhere in the editor.

Edit: It looks like it needs to update on scene change for `highlight_scene_scripts`.

summary: Minor - C# "Missing Connected Method" warning doesn't disappear after fixing the issue.

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in 4.5.1 RC 1 ### System information Godot v4.5.1.rc1.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8088) - AMD Ryzen 9 5950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 127.9 GiB memory ### Issue description If you create an Area2D signal (via "signals" in the editor) and with C# code, the c# code editor (in Godot) tells you on compile that there is a missing connected method (as it should) However, if you ADD the correct method, the warning doesnt go away (even though the signal now happens). Create An object, add a C# Script. Create an Area2D as a child, and set up collision shape/etc as normal On the Area2D, go to "Node", then "Signals" Under "body_entered" Create a new signal You'll be told: **C# : Callback code won't be generated, please add it manually** Now press "connect" Go to the C# Script in the godot editor, and hit build. You'll see the warning: **_Missing connected method 'FunctionName' for signal 'body_entered' from node 'Node2D/CharacterBody2D/HitBox' to node ''Node2D/CharacterBody2D'_** (or similar, depending on how you set up your tree) Now, add the missing FunctionName, etc. And build again. the code will now work, and you'll have zero warnings, in the build window. But the warning window will still show "Missing connected method" I've added a visual here: Red #1 : Function Name added Red #2 : Callback happening (can now play sound effect) Red #3 : Warning disappeared from build Red #4 : Still seeing the warning message, with the warning number (i.e. the yellow triangle with !) in the warning window. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) will do later, it's a minor issue for me.. But something which is confusing at first.

summary: Adjust panning multipliers

description.text:
Fixes #106168

Scope: fixes Linux trackpad scrolling on Wayland (previously way too fast), and fixes(?) MacOS trackpad scrolling (previously would get slower for smaller screens?). Windows is unchanged, and mouse scrolling on Linux/MacOS is unchanged.

On Wayland, get_delta() returns a value intended to be interpreted as a measure of some amount of pixels, however previously it was being multiplied by the page size, which makes no sense; the delta is not a proportion of the page size. This has been fixed.

As for MacOS, get_delta() returns a value that is intended to be used to "scroll by the returned amount." Based on some quick math I believe it's a measure of pixels similar to what Wayland uses, meaning that this PR is also fixing an unnoticed bug with scrolling on MacOS (small screen sizes shouldn't and no longer cause scrolling to be more sluggish), although I don't have a Mac to test this on.

Relevant docs:
    https://developer.apple.com/documentation/appkit/nsevent/scrollingdeltay
    https://developer.apple.com/documentation/appkit/nsevent/scrollingdeltax

This PR will probably result in some changes to the scroll speed on MacOS, and I don't have a Macbook with a trackpad to test it on. By some rough math it shouldn't change significantly, but it's difficult to measure because previously the scroll speed depended on the size of the window; splitting the screen one app on top and Godot on the bottom could cut the scroll speed of individual panels into thirds or less.

summary: Editor: Fix 'More Info…' link in Export/Encryption

description.text:
<!--
Please target the `master` branch in priority.

Relevant fixes are cherry-picked for stable branches as needed by maintainers.

To speed up the contribution process and avoid CI errors, please set up pre-commit hooks locally:
https://contributing.godotengine.org/en/latest/engine/guidelines/code_style.html
-->
Problem
The More Info… button in Project → Export → [any preset] → Encryption opens a 404.
The link still points to the old docs path:
.../contributing/development/compiling/compiling_with_script_encryption_key.html

Solution
Update the path to the current location in the docs:
.../engine_details/development/compiling/compiling_with_script_encryption_key.html

The URL is built with GODOT_VERSION_DOCS_URL, so it resolves to the running editor’s version (e.g. en/latest for dev builds, or en/4.5 for stable).

Changed file

editor/export/project_export.cpp (1 line)

Testing
- Built the editor on Windows.
- Opened any project → Project → Export… → Add… → Windows Desktop → Encryption tab → More Info….
- Browser opens the “Compiling with PCK encryption key” page under engine_details/....
- Verified both dev (en/latest) and stable (en/4.x) builds resolve correctly.

Risk
Very low; editor-only change, no runtime impact.

Fixes
Fixes #111108.

summary: Add the ExternalLink icon to the "Update available" text

description.text: (Here I have the minor version set to 5, but it shows my version as being 4.6, I don't know why this happens, but it doesn't change the bahavior of this PR as its an unrelated bug.) summary: DisplayServer.dialog_show does nothing in exported projects on Windows

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.5-stable not reproducible in 4.4.1


### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility)

### Issue description

`DisplayServer.dialog_show` can be used on OSes that have this feature (like Windows and macOS) to show a native OS style dialog popup with options. Useful for confirmation or unsaved changes style dialogs. 

However, in 4.5 this has stopped working on Windows in exported projects, and instead it does nothing. No errors, just nothing happens. It still works as far as I can tell on macOS in exported builds and it also still works as expected on Windows when running the project from the editor.

While `dialog_show `might not be used that often this is quite a serious issue if you're using it since it's hard to see, as it doesn't show up when testing from the editor. And it can lock your projects in invalid states as prompts like this often put apps in a modal state where it expects the dialog callbacks to be called to proceed.


### Steps to reproduce

Have a simple setup project where pressing a button calls the dialog_show. For example:

```
func _on_button_pressed() -> void:
	DisplayServer.dialog_show("Test","Message",["Button 1","Button 2"],dialog_callback)
	
func dialog_callback(selected_button:int) -> void:
	print("dialog_callback selected_option:"+str(selected_button))
```

Run project from editor and press button to observe it works.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/28ab9875-6a5f-4398-8d7a-31d33b3ed507

Export the project (with all default settings) on windows and run the exported project
Pressing the button nothing happens, also included output console to show there are no errors when this happens.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d3e89af8-d119-4ee3-b5a2-670c5ecca06d

summary: UI and viewport scale become incorrect when using Stretch (any mode) across monitors with different DPI scaling

description.text: ### Tested versions Godot 4.5 stable ### System information Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 11 - Multi-window, 2 monitors - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop (Driver 566.26, DCH) - OpenGL(Compatibility) - AMD Ryzen 7 7735HS with Radeon Graphics (3.20 GHz) - ### Issue description When using multiple monitors with different DPI scaling factors (for example, one at 125% and another at 100%), any project that has Stretch enabled (`viewport `or `canvas_items` mode) will cause the UI and viewport scale to become distorted or offset after moving the editor or the running game window between screens. **Stretch disabled:** **Stretch canvas_items:** The issue occurs consistently — even if `Allow hiDPI `is disabled. Once the distortion happens, it persists across restarts, even if: - Cache is cleared - Godot is reinstalled - System is rebooted - Running a copy of the project Only setting Stretch → Mode to disabled prevents the problem, but this also disables all automatic resizing and scaling behavior. ### Steps to reproduce I can no longer reliably reproduce this issue in new projects after rebooting the system, but my current project remains broken and continues to show the incorrect UI and viewport scaling behavior. Possible reproduction steps: 1. Use a multi-monitor setup with different DPI scaling (e.g., main monitor 125%, secondary 100%). 2. Create a new project with any Control-based UI. 3. summary: Expose ScriptEditor::is_editor_floating() to GDScript

description.text:
A very simple PR(my first) to expose the `is_editor_floating()` method to GDScript for use by `EditorPlugin`s. I'm using it for a plugin to add VIM-like behavior to the Script Editor.

This is necessary because when the Script Editor is floating, it will still receive shortcuts from an `EditorPlugin` even if it doesn't have focus. Having access to `is_editor_floating()` allows me to flip the processing logic of the `NOTIFICATION_WM_WINDOW_FOCUS_OUT`/`IN` notifications to reliably handle or not handle shortcuts in the floating Script Editor window since there don't appear to be any dedicated notifications for the floating window itself.

summary: Generated Trimesh CollisionShape3D for a cube gets lost in the center of Area3D with CylinderShape3D

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducable in v4.5.stable.official [876b29033], 4.4.1, 4.3.stable ### System information Godot v4.5.stable - macOS Sonoma (14.7.8) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M3 Max (Apple9) - Apple M3 Max (16 threads) - 64.00 GiB memory ### Issue description This is gonna be a weird one. I was doing basic testing on setting up interactable items, and used a standard 1-meter cube, and generated the collision shape using the "Create Collision Shape" menu. Then I used an Area3D, with a CyllinderShape3D to detect it. I quickly discovered that the Area3D would emit `body_exited` when the StaticBody3D neared the center of the Area3D. I threw together a quick MRP and wound that the Area3D with a CyllinderShape3D with a radius < 2.0 will "lose" a 1 meter cube StaticBody3D when the body approaches the center of the CyllinderShape3D. The `body_exited` signal is emitted, and the cube is no longer listed in `Area3D.get_overlapping_bodies()`. In this video of the MRP, you'll see the cube enter the Area3D and turn yellow, but when it reaches the center, it will turn white because the `body_exited` signal has been issued. It will return to yellow as the cube exits the center of the cyllinder, then white when it actually exits the Area3D. After that I show the problem isn't present when the radius is 2.0. I then use the sliders to show that the issue is also present at slower speeds. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0684cfdd-83fc-43c2-933a-53766d9e4012 I played around a little more, and found that if I manually set a BoxShape, it works fine. Also, the other options for generating the collision shape work fine. This issue only happens with a Trimesh generated Shape2D. ### Steps to reproduce If you're using the MRP: * Run the scene * Move the radius slider to change the size of the Area3D cyllinder * values < 2.0 will show the cube exiting the Area3D when it approaches the center of the cyllinder * values >= 2.0 will not demonstrate the problem * Changing the duration slider speeds up how fast the Area3D ping-pongs * Changing this slider does not resolve the issue, so it doesn't look like a speed issue If you want to do things manually: To create the shape causing the problem: * Create a StaticBody3D * Add a MeshInstance3D child with the default Cube mesh * Use the Mesh -> Create Collision Shape; Sibilng, Trimesh To create the Area3D detector: * Create Area3D * Add CollisionShape3D with CyllinderShape, radius 1.75, height 2 ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [area-3d-body-exit-issue.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22703054/area-3d-body-exit-issue.zip)

summary: Check that ColorPickerButton popup is currently open in `_modal_closed()`

description.text:
Fixes #111245 

This is my first time contributing here so please let me know if I've done something wrong.

Currently the `_modal_closed()` method checks if `ui_cancel` was just pressed so it can revert the color to the old one before closing. This wasn't an issue before as previously this method was only called when the popup was closed, but after #109824 it is also possible for this method to be called when the ```ColorPickerButton``` exits the scene tree, regardless of if the popup was already open; and since `ui_cancel` can deselect the current node from the inspector it is possible for `_modal_closed()` to be called on the same frame as `ui_cancel` without the popup previously being open.

I've fixed this by just adding a check to see if the popup is currently visible in the scene tree, which seems to work from my testing but there might be some edge cases where the popup is hidden before `_modal_closed()` is called, so let me know if there is a more robust solution this. This also fixes another potential issue where the `popup_closed` signal would be called on scene tree exit too, even while it was already closed. 

I have also tested the MRP from the issue that #109824 was fixing and confirmed that it still works.

description.text:
[godot.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22697492/godot.log)

### Tested versions

v4.5.stable.mono.official

### System information

Windows 10 - OpenGL API 3.3.0 NVIDIA 576.02 - Compatibility - Using Device: NVIDIA - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060

### Issue description

Projects using the .NET 10 Release Candidate (10.0.0-rc.1.25451.107) fail to run when launched from the Godot 4.5 editor's "Play" button. The editor's output log is spammed with the error .NET: Failed to load project assembly.
This appears to be an issue specific to the Godot IDE's integration with .NET 10, as the project exhibits the following behaviors:

1. The exported project runs correctly wihtout errors.
2. The project can be successfully run and debugged from an external IDE (Visual Studio).
3. The issue is resolved immediately by retargeting the project back to .NET 8. The project then runs correctly from within the Godot editor.

This points to a problem with how the editor is handling the build, assembly loading, or hot-reloading process for the new .NET 10 SDK, rather than a fundamental incompatibility with the engine's runtime.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new Godot 4.5 project using the mono version of the engine.
2. Add a simple scene (e.g., a Control node for a GUI).
3. Add a TextEdit node to the scene.
4. Attach a new C# script to the root node.
5. Save the scene and script. At this point, the project targets .NET 8 by default and runs correctly when the "Play" button is pressed.
6. Modify the .csproj file to retarget the project to .NET 10 RC. Change the <TargetFramework> from net8.0 to net10.0.
7. Return to the Godot editor. The project will re-import the assemblies.
8. Press the "Play" button. The project will fail to launch, and the output console will show the errors below.

Error Log
```Error Log
--- Debug adapter server started on port 6006 ---
  ERROR: modules/mono/mono_gd/gd_mono.cpp:801 - .NET: Failed to load project assembly.
--- GDScript language server started on port 6005 ---
```

summary: Fix tooltip alignment: flip and clamp to keep inside viewport

description.text:
This PR fixes an issue where tooltips could render partially off-screen near
the right or bottom edges of the viewport.

### Changes
- Updated `Viewport::_gui_show_tooltip_at` in `scene/main/viewport.cpp`.
- Tooltips are first positioned offset from the cursor.
- If they would overflow the viewport, they are flipped to the opposite side (left/above).
- If flipping still fails, tooltips are clamped inside the viewport edges.

### Testing
- Verified tooltip behavior at different Editor Display Scales (0.75×, 1.0×, 1.5×, 2.0×).
- Tested on Windows with display scaling at 125% and 150%.
- Tooltips remain fully visible and no longer overlap the mouse cursor.

### Before
- Tooltips sometimes went off-screen or covered the cursor.

### After
- Tooltips now offset correctly, flip when needed, and clamp as fallback.

summary: Add custom tooltip project setting and custom tooltip option to viewport

description.text: Adds the `gui/common/custom_tooltip_scene` ProjectSetting that can be used to define a scene to be used for tooltips project wide. This will more easily permit the use of tooltips, especially in cases where the `Control` node generating tooltips uses a `Window` in some way (such as the `OptionButton`'s popup menu) as in those cases just overridding `_make_custom_tooltip` for the button does not do it. Whenever a `Viewport` is created, it will set its `gui.custom_tooltip` to the project setting. Additionally, `Viewport` now has the `set_custom_tooltip` function to change the tooltips for that `Viewport`. As a note this will not affect any controls like `OptionButton`'s popup menu though, as `Window` has it's own separate viewport, that's why the project setting exists. This allows the use of custom tooltips with much less friction, removing the need to either make class implementations that override `_make_custom_tooltip` for many nodes (and more serious work for those like `OptionButton` or `Tree`/`ItemList` to handle the internal controls) or the need to apply a script to *every single control* that you want to use your custom tooltip. Now you can just set a project setting. Or if you have a viewport that should have specific types of tooltips, you can set it for that viewport with one line of code. As a note the logic in viewport for what tooltip is generated is now: 1) Call `_make_custom_tooltip` on the requesting control 2) If above did not return anything, check if the viewport has one defined in `gui.custom_tooltip` set either to the project setting if it has a value when the viewport was created, or when the user calls `set_custom_tooltip` 3) If the above did not set it, default to the engine provided label. This means you can still use `_make_custom_tooltip` if you wish, and this PR does not change any existing behaviour. Closes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/13325 # Example: **Download:** [custom-tooltip-eval-project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22688803/custom-tooltip-eval-project.zip) There is an editor setting override, as without it the editor will also use those custom tooltips which may be undesired. This also means you could set custom editor tooltips easily if you wanted. The current editor tooltips (where used) don't have a saved scene though and are constructed purely in code. Video: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d7a9fc1f-5a0e-490a-91c6-4b212b88756e The tooltip with a squid icon is the scene that the `gui/common/custom_tooltip_scene` is set to in this demo project. The scene that the "Set custom tooltip" CheckButton enables is just a RichTextLabel with no icon. The one icon that says "A custom tooltip set" uses a godot logo icon instead of the squid, set using `_make_custom_tooltip` to demonstrate that takes priority. The "Set custom tooltip" CheckButton, when toggled off, calls `set_custom_tooltip` with null, which reverts back to default engine behavior of providing the base label. The fallback checkbox changes it to use the project setting scene instead. Since the viewport sets it's value to the project setting only when constructed, if you ever override it you would need to handle setting it back to that project setting if you wanted it back to using that. I have also tested using C# in a separate project, but the provided demo only uses gdscript for ease of review.

summary: Unmapped flight joystick -> gamepad buttons/axes are ignored and not usable despite being supported by SDL

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5 as well as 06827c91c6ee98068a48c66be0cc067ebb434a2f

### System information

Godot v4.6.dev.mono (06827c91c) - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon RX 9070 (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.21016.4) - AMD Ryzen 7 5700G with Radeon Graphics (16 threads) - 63.85 GiB memory

### Issue description

I have a flight joystick which is mapped in the controller database, a Thrustmaster T.16000M. However, buttons/axes that are not mapped to gamepad buttons/axes are simply ignored.

I was able to track _why_ this is happening: the joystick mapping causes `SDL_IsGamepad(...)` to return `true` for this input device, and the `SKIP_EVENT_FOR_GAMEPAD` in `drivers/sdl/joypad_sdl.cpp` simply skips these events. However, this is undesirable behaviour for a few main reasons:
1. This makes it impossible, without unmapping these controllers in the gamepad database, to use these button/axes for stuff such as flight simulation or general joystick input.
2. This is confusing to end users of games with configurable input systems, as these buttons/axes are simply nonfunctional in seemingly random ways depending on the gamepad configuration.
3. The gamepad axis code manually remaps certain axes from -1..1 to 0..1 which is not what all users may want.

These inputs are supported by SDL, almost certainly on all platforms (excluding perhaps XInput).

An easy solution to rectify this is to remove non-gamepad mappings (i.e. mappings for flight hardware/HOTAS) from the `gamecontrollerdb.txt` file, however this may be sub-optimal for certain users of the engine or games running on it depending on use-case.

It may be necessary to define some sort of input management flag that tells the input system to use joystick/gamepad-like controllers as a joystick rather than a gamepad, either globally or on a per-controller basis. With this flag, the input system should use `SDL_EVENT_JOYSTICK_*` events instead of `SDL_EVENT_GAMEPAD_*` events and ignore the duplicate gamepad events, or vice-versa when the flag is disabled. However, I'm not sure how this should be implemented due to my inexperience with Godot's internal codebase hence why I haven't tried to implement it myself and make a PR.

I believe #84770 is related, specifically [the yaw not working on v4.5.beta7.official](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/84770#issuecomment-3268421565).

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the Joypad demo project.
2. Run the program and select a flight joystick which is mapped in the controller database but has more axes/buttons than are mapped. I used a Thrustmaster T.16000M for this example, GUID `030065a94f0400000ab1000000000000`, matching under `030000004f0400000ab1000000000000,T16000M,a:b0,b:b1,back:b12,dpdown:h0.4,dpleft:h0.8,dpright:h0.2,dpup:h0.1,guide:b11,leftshoulder:b4,lefttrigger:b9,leftx:a0,lefty:a1,rightshoulder:b6,righttrigger:b7,start:b10,x:b2,y:b3,platform:Windows,` in [`gamecontrollerdb.txt`](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/06827c91c6ee98068a48c66be0cc067ebb434a2f/core/input/gamecontrollerdb.txt).
3. Press all buttons and move all axes and observe that some axis movement/button presses are ignored.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I used the [Joypads demo](https://godotengine.org/asset-library/asset/140) to test this issue.

summary: Wireframe rendering of Joint3D and their angular limits do not match runtime behaviour.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 

- Godot 4.5
- Godot 4.5 .NET 
- Custom build of Godot 4.5 that enables hiding of Joint3D nodes based on visibility.

### System information

Linux Mint 22.2 - Godot v4.5 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2070 SUPER Mobile / Max-Q (TU104M)

### Issue description

In the Godot 3D editor, the wireframe display of the HingeJoint3D nodes do not always reflect their runtime behaviour, leading to confusion and frustration setting up complex articulated 3D physics constructs.

I understand that the order of `node_a` and `node_b` matter and that the limits are defined relative to `node_a`. However the wireframe display doesn't seem to change regardless of which `RigidBody3D` is used to configure `node_a`.

### Steps to reproduce

- Create and configure two `RigidBody3D` nodes.
- Connect them using a `HingeJoint3D` and enable its Angular Limits.
- Configure one of the angular limits to 0 for demontration purposes.
- Attach the first body to `node_a` and the second body to `node_b`.
- Observe the wireframe rendering of the `Joint3D` and runtime behaviour.
- Now swap the order, attach the second body to `node_a` and the first body to `node_b`.
- Observe the wireframe rendering of the `Joint3D` and runtime behaviour.
- The wireframe renders the same but the runtime behaviour is different.
- Alternatively checkout the demo project below.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I've created a small Godot project which reproduces the issue and provides some suggestions on how to resolve. This is available over at Github: https://github.com/jdarc/Godot-Joints-Confusion

summary: Add global resources filesystem

description.text:
Implement the first part of my [Global plugin proposal](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/831#issuecomment-3355144722), which added a new global resource file system that can be access from all projects.

The resources, custom classes, and add-ons that are placed in the global directory will load into the editor like the normal project resources.
You can access the directory using the `global://` directory prefix.
The directory is placed in the godot data folder  (`.../godot/global_resources/4.6/`).

## Usage:
- Launch the .exe file to generate the global directory folder.
- Then go to the godot data folder (you can easily find it in your project under `editor->open editor data/settings folder`)
- Once in the data folder, navigate to `global_resources/4.6/` and place your resources/addons there.
- At first, you might get errors and have to restart your project **multiple times**, but after that, it should load like normal resources.

## Tested environment

### Operating system:
Windows 11. Other operating systems are not tested and maybe not even implemented yet.
 
### Addons:
I tested the PR with these add-ons, and it seems to work fine:
- [Inspector-Tabs](https://godotengine.org/asset-library/asset/3951)
- [Script-IDE](https://godotengine.org/asset-library/asset/2206)

## TODO

- [ ] Fix having to reload the project multiple time.

summary: Fix 'LineEdit' Unicode code-point/control character insertion failing to emit text_changed

description.text:
### Summary
`LineEdit` did not emit `text_changed` when text was inserted via Unicode code-point input and the “Insert Control Character” menu. This would break UI logic that depends on `text_changed`.

### Reproduction
Minimal scene with a single `LineEdit` element and the following attached script:

```Python
extends LineEdit
func _ready():
    text_changed.connect(func(new_text):
        print("text_changed:", new_text))
```

Pressing ctrl + shift + u and typing `2020` then pressing enter inserts the dagger character (†) but does not trigger `text_changed` i.e. no print statement is written to the console.

Right click + Insert Control Character (pick any) also inserts but does **not** trigger `text_changed`.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7b3ac2a4-2b5b-4b6c-adad-1ac9fc9d4ea1

### Fix
After performing the two aforementioned insertions, defer `_text_changed()` and set the `text_changed_dirty` guard (exact same process used in `paste_text()` ). Regular typing and paste behavior remains unaffected. `set_text()` is unchanged and still does **not** emit, as per the documentation. This is a small, localized fix in `scene/gui/line_edit.cpp`

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6d11362b-e9e6-4047-97b9-8e55f7624c5b

### Testing
Verified fix on a Windows dev build, code-point submission and control character insertion now print once to the console and update any bound UI elements. Regular typing, paste, and deletions have been verified to still emit exactly once.

Formatting: clang-format 17

Fixes #110809

Note: There is an overlapping PR #110988 that instead moves the `text_changed` emission logic to the core function `insert_text_at_caret()` while removing `text.length() != prev_len` checks to determine whether to emit `text_changed` or not. Let me know if this more general change to a core function is preferred, as the changes in my PR attempt to fix two specific bugs without altering core functions while aligning with the existing architecture. A previous PR #84382 also attempted to alter `insert_text_at_caret()` directly which resulted in duplicate signals.

summary: Add Auto update check mode

description.text: Should resolve #111161 Currently the `check_for_updates` has an awkward "default" that changes depending on whether you use stable version or not. Which means if you switch between versions, the setting might get reset or get some invalid value or whatever. This PR adds an explicit Auto version check mode, which is also the new default. The name changes depending on whether you use stable and it works like the current default, but if you change `check_for_updates` to e.g. Newest Stable, it will stay like that even if you use a preview version. I also replaced `get_version_info()` in EngineUpdateLabel with proper `version.h` usage.
